info.title: Admin API
info.description: Admin endpoints for managing peers and server resources.

tags.name: Resources
tags.description: Declarative admin resource get, put, and delete operations

tags.description: Business resource asset upload and download operations

tags.description: Admin read-only peer gameplay runtime inspection

tags.name: WorkspaceHistory
tags.description: Workspace history metadata and audio assets

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: GizClaw system-log filter, limited to 4096 UTF-8 bytes. Use * or up to 32 AND-separated clauses: level:value, text:value, field:value, field!=value, field:*, or -field:*. Values are unquoted tokens or JSON string literals; level names are normalized to uppercase. Provider query syntax and metadata fields, wildcards inside values, OR, regex, and stream/kind/message overrides are not accepted.

parameters.name: start_time_ms
parameters.description: Inclusive search start time in Unix milliseconds. Required for the first page.

parameters.name: end_time_ms
parameters.description: Exclusive search end time in Unix milliseconds. Required for the first page.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum log records emitted by this stream page. Defaults to 100 and is clamped by the server.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque GizClaw cursor returned by the previous end event. Cursor-only continuation is supported; repeated filter, time, or order fields must match, while limit may change.

200.description: Server-sent event stream of log, end, and post-start error events.

schema.description: SSE stream event payload. event: log uses ServerLogEntry JSON data; event: end uses ServerLogStreamEnd JSON data; event: error uses ErrorResponse JSON data.

400.description: Invalid log query parameters or cursor

501.description: Log query backend is not configured

502.description: Backend search failed before the stream started

post.description: Create or update a declarative admin resource described by apiVersion, kind, metadata, and spec.

501.description: Apply is defined but not implemented

get.description: Get a named declarative admin resource by kind and name.

501.description: Resource get is defined but not implemented

put.summary: Create or update an admin resource
put.description: Create or update a named declarative admin resource. The request body's kind and metadata.name must match the path parameters.

501.description: Resource put is defined but not implemented

delete.description: Delete a named declarative admin resource by kind and name. ResourceList and resources without delete semantics are rejected.

put.summary: Create or update a firmware release line

post.description: Atomically release by shifting slots left in develop, beta, stable, pending order: beta to develop, stable to beta, pending to stable, then clear pending. The resulting stable slot must be non-empty.

Atomically rollback by shifting slots right in develop, beta, stable, pending order: develop to beta, beta to stable, stable to pending, then clear develop. The resulting stable slot must be non-empty.

409.description: Workspace already exists or is pending deletion

delete.description: Immediately removes a user-created Workspace and writes a durable pending-deletion handoff. System Workspaces cannot be deleted through this operation; physical runtime, history, assets, objects, and files are retained for asynchronous cleanup.

409.description: System Workspace deletion is forbidden through the generic Workspace lifecycle

get.summary: Get latest sampled telemetry values for a peer

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Comma-separated telemetry field names. Omitted means all supported fields.

get.summary: Query sampled telemetry points for a peer

parameters.name: step_ms
parameters.description: Range evaluation step in milliseconds. Omitted derives a bounded step from the requested range and limit.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum returned points. Omitted uses the default point budget.

get.summary: Download a firmware channel artifact tar
get.description: Download the original artifact.tar uploaded for a firmware channel.

404.description: Firmware, channel, or artifact not found

put.summary: Upload a firmware channel artifact tar
put.description: Upload artifact.tar for a firmware channel. The server stores the original tar, extracts regular files, writes a manifest, and updates server-owned channel artifact metadata. Existing package objects cause a conflict until deleted.

delete.description: Delete artifact.tar, extracted files, manifest, and channel artifact metadata.

404.description: Firmware, channel, or artifact not found

get.description: List one file entry or the immediate children of a directory inside the channel artifact manifest.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Directory or file path. Empty means artifact root.

404.description: Firmware, channel, artifact, or path not found

get.description: Return recursive flat entries below a directory in the channel artifact manifest.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Directory path. Empty means artifact root.

404.description: Firmware, channel, artifact, or path not found

get.summary: Stat a firmware artifact or entry
get.description: Return artifact-level stats when path is empty, otherwise return one file or directory entry stat.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: File or directory path. Empty means artifact-level stats.

404.description: Firmware, channel, artifact, or path not found

get.description: Download one regular file extracted from the channel artifact.

404.description: Firmware, channel, artifact, or path not found
